blocks.content: You'll learn how to immediately halt a rollout if a pipeline causes problems. Fleet Management's **Active** toggle lets you stop delivery to all matched collectors in one click — no attribute changes or pipeline deletion needed.
To roll back a pipeline, complete the following steps:
In the **Remote configuration** list, locate the **Active** toggle next to the pipeline you want to halt.

Click the **Active** toggle to deactivate the pipeline. Fleet Management stops sending this configuration to matched collectors on their next poll.

You don't need to change matching attributes or delete the pipeline — the deactivated pipeline stays in the list so you can reactivate it or use it as a reference when troubleshooting.

> **Tip:** Use Fleet Management's [**Pipelines history**](/docs/grafana-cloud/send-data/fleet-management/manage-fleet/pipelines/view-pipeline-history/) feature to review what changed and restore a previous version if needed.

You now have a complete and repeatable strategy for safely rolling out configuration pipelines. Use the `env` attribute and regex matching to control the blast radius of each change, and the **Active** toggle as your emergency stop.
